# Governance

This repository follows a transparent, meritocratic governance model.

## 📌 Roles

- **Owner** - An owner has complete administrative access to an organisation or repository.

    - They manage all organisation/repository settings, security, and billing.
    - Adding and removing members or collaborators.
    - Promoting or demoting members/maintainers.
    - Deleting repositories or the entire organisation.
    - They also often have the responsibilities of a **maintainer**.

- **Maintainer**: A maintainer is usually a project managers who manages the repository without access to certain sensitive or destructive actions (like billing, deleting repositories, etc.).

    - They can manage repository settings, control team access, and enforce branch protection rules.
    - In a project context, a maintainer is generally seen as having a part in the "ownership" of the project direction and success.
    - Maintainers are active members of the project.

- **Collaborator**: A collaborator is a user (either a project member or an "outside collaborator") who has been granted direct access to the repository.

    - Collaborators typically have write access, meaning they can push commits directly to the repository (subject to branch protection rules) and are expected to actively work on the project's codebase.
    - They often work with maintainers to have their code merged into the main codebase.

- **Contributor**: This is a broad, informal term for anyone whose commits are part of a repository's Git history.

    - They contribute via forks and pull requests (PRs), but also (in terms of metrics) issues.
    - A contributor does not necessarily have any special access permissions to the repository's settings or even the right to push code directly.

## 🗳 Decision Process

1. Ideas are proposed as issues or pull requests.
2. Discussion normally happens publicly on GitHub.
3. Maintainers resolve disagreements by consensus; when needed, a core maintainer or owner has final say.

## ✨ How to Become a Maintainer

- Contribute regularly with high-quality code or reviews.
- Demonstrate understanding of project goals and best practices.
- Have the availably to maintain the project.
- Nomination via existing maintainers or owners.

## 🧾 Releases

- Releases are made from `main`.
- Versioning follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
